description: An action embedding timestamps or unstable ordering produces different outputs from identical inputs, poisoning the shared cache.
body: Failure mode: an output bakes in a build timestamp, absolute path, or nondeterministic iteration order, so two builds of the same inputs differ. Detection signals: byte diffs between repeated builds; cache never hits for the action; downstream targets rebuild needlessly. Do not accept outputs that vary without input changes. Safer fallback: strip timestamps, sort collections, use stable relative paths, and confirm byte-identical outputs across repeated and cross-machine builds before trusting the cache.
